title: Huttonella prona
intro: Huttonella prona is a species of fungus that belongs to the family Trichocomaceae, commonly found in soil and decaying organic matter. The health benefits of Huttonella prona include its potential to exhibit antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which may aid in the treatment of various infections and diseases. The bioactive constituents of Huttonella prona include secondary metabolites such as citrinin and other compounds that have been shown to possess antimicrobial and antitumor activities. In traditional medicine, Huttonella prona is often used in herbal preparations, such as extracts and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including skin infections and digestive disorders.
taxonomy: Huttonella prona is a species of bacteria within the family Huttonellaceae. Huttonella prona is classified under the phylum Bacteroidota. It is a Gram-negative, rod-shaped bacterium. This classification is based on its morphological and biochemical characteristics. Huttonella prona is a chemoorganotrophic, facultative anaerobe, which means it can live with or without oxygen.
